text.title: MegaLinter as the Polyglot Quality Umbrella in CI Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Polyglot repo (≥ 3 languages) MUST run MegaLinter as ONE CI job, not N hand-rolled linter jobs.
rule.rationale: Per-language jobs drift; single MegaLinter ensures consistent versioning across languages.

rule.statement: .mega-linter.yml MUST be committed with explicit enable list (or disable list) and per-language excludes.
rule.rationale: Default config enables too many linters; explicit selection prevents noise.

rule.statement: MegaLinter MUST emit SARIF; CI uploads SARIF to GitHub code-scanning (or equivalent).
rule.rationale: SARIF feeds the security tab + lets findings persist across PRs.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST pin a specific MegaLinter flavour image SHA (e.g. oxsecurity/megalinter@sha256:...).
rule.rationale: Floating tags change behaviour; pinned SHA is reproducible.

rule.statement: MegaLinter is the CI floor; local pre-commit hooks remain the developer floor (don't replace one with the other).
rule.rationale: Local hooks are fast feedback; CI is the merge gate. Replacing locals with image pull is too slow.

rule.statement: If repo is single-language OR CI cannot pull Docker images OR repo too small, STOP.
rule.rationale: MegaLinter's value is polyglot consolidation; outside that, cost > value.
